facts.description: The current approved exhibit title must appear as the main poster title.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Exact approved_current wording 'Tides of Memory' should be readable in the final image; minor punctuation/spacing differences are acceptable only if the wording remains unambiguous.

facts.description: The current approved subtitle must appear near the title.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Exact approved_current wording 'A harbor history exhibition' should be readable in the final image; minor punctuation/spacing differences are acceptable only if the wording remains unambiguous.

facts.description: The current approved date line must appear in the lower information area.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Exact approved_current wording 'May 3 - August 18' should be readable in the final image; minor punctuation/spacing differences are acceptable only if the wording remains unambiguous.

facts.description: The current approved CTA must appear as the poster call to action.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Exact approved_current wording 'Reserve opening weekend' should be readable in the final image; minor punctuation/spacing differences are acceptable only if the wording remains unambiguous.

facts.description: The artifact should use exact 1080 x 1350 px canvas size.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Image metadata should be 1080 by 1350 pixels; this is separate from aspect ratio.

facts.description: The artifact must have 4:5 aspect ratio.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Rendered output has 4:5 aspect ratio; visible crop should not be landscape or another format.

facts.description: The poster must use the current deep harbor blue, warm paper, and copper museum palette.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Dominant visual system should clearly follow the current palette from env; exact pixel-level hex matching is not required.

facts.description: The current Harborlight Museum mark must sit in the upper-left safe area.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Current logo/mark treatment is visibly placed in the upper-left safe area and is not replaced by a deprecated mark.

facts.description: Title and subtitle occupy the upper title area with museum-poster hierarchy.
facts.expected_value: Title and subtitle occupy the upper title area with museum-poster hierarchy.
facts.semantic_acceptance: The title/headline area is visually clear and follows the current layout zone.

facts.description: Date line and CTA appear in the lower poster area.
facts.expected_value: Date line and CTA appear in the lower poster area.
facts.semantic_acceptance: CTA and related lower information are visibly placed in the required CTA zone.

facts.description: Essential copy, logo, and CTA stay inside the 64 px safe margin.
facts.semantic_acceptance: No essential text, logo, or CTA is clipped, flush to the edge, or outside a visibly safe area.

facts.description: The current template zones must be visible and coherent.
expected_value: central archival harbor or map-inspired hero zone
facts.semantic_acceptance: The composition visibly uses the current zones listed in env/template.yaml and visual_rules.json.

facts.description: Hero visual includes archival harbor-history imagery or treatment.
facts.semantic_acceptance: The hero visual must show this component clearly enough for a viewer to recognize: archival harbor-history visual visible.

facts.description: Hero visual includes a historic port map, nautical artifact, coordinate grid, or museum object cue.
facts.expected_value: map, nautical artifact, or museum object cue visible
facts.semantic_acceptance: The hero visual must show this component clearly enough for a viewer to recognize: map, nautical artifact, or museum object cue visible.

facts.description: Visual treatment reads as a museum exhibition, not tourism or aquarium advertising.
facts.semantic_acceptance: The hero visual must show this component clearly enough for a viewer to recognize: museum exhibition context visible.

facts.description: Hero avoids horror shipwreck spectacle and uses refined archival restraint.
facts.semantic_acceptance: The hero visual must show this component clearly enough for a viewer to recognize: refined non-horror harbor-history cue visible.

facts.description: Deprecated assets, templates, stale slogans, wrong campaign copy, and forbidden directions must not be used as active final design elements.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Deprecated items may appear only in explicit do-not-use/deprecated/excluded context, never as active final design elements.

facts.description: Old or deprecated slogan/copy 'Shipwreck Screams' must not be used as active headline, subtitle, supporting line, CTA, or claim.
facts.semantic_acceptance: The old copy may only appear in explicit exclusion/deprecated context, not as active final poster text.

facts.description: Old logo/mark treatment 'Harborlight Kids Wave mascot' must not be used as the primary logo or brand mark.
facts.semantic_acceptance: The old logo/mark may only appear in explicit exclusion/deprecated context, not as the active visible logo.

facts.description: The image must not add unsupported guarantees, discounts, free offers, safety promises, or exaggerated performance claims not present in approved_current copy.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Forbidden if presented as active marketing copy; acceptable only if clearly shown as draft/test/do-not-use material.

facts.description: The final image should maintain refined, archival, accessible harbor-history museum style.
facts.expected_value: refined, archival, accessible harbor-history museum style
facts.semantic_acceptance: Overall visible style should match the current brand tone and avoid deprecated genre cues.

facts.description: Neon aquarium palette must not be used as the active color system.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Forbidden only when presented as an active final design element; explicit do-not-use/deprecated/excluded mentions are allowed.

facts.description: Harborlight Kids Wave mascot or cartoon sea animal must not be used as hero or brand character.
allowed_patterns: Harborlight Kids Wave mascot.{0,120}(deprecated|do not use|excluded|forbidden|retired|old)
facts.semantic_acceptance: Forbidden only when presented as an active final design element; explicit do-not-use/deprecated/excluded mentions are allowed.

facts.description: Storm horror template must not be used as layout basis, hero, background, or primary typography.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Forbidden only when presented as an active final design element; explicit do-not-use/deprecated/excluded mentions are allowed.

calculation_notes: All expected facts are derived from task-visible env files. The final copy is selected by choosing the approved_current row in env/campaign_brief.md and rejecting archived, draft, test, and deprecated rows. Platform specs require 1080 x 1350 px and 4:5 aspect ratio with 64 px safe margin. Current layout requires upper-left mark zone, top title and subtitle zone, central archival harbor or map-inspired hero zone, lower date and CTA zone. Hero visual must include: archival harbor-history visual visible, map, nautical artifact, or museum object cue visible, museum exhibition context visible, refined non-horror harbor-history cue visible. Deprecated exclusions are role-based: Harborlight Kids Wave mascot, Shipwreck Screams, neon aquarium palette, storm horror template, Neon Reef Nights; they may be mentioned only in explicit excluded/deprecated/do-not-use context and must not be active final design elements. Unsupported claim examples rejected from draft/test copy: free admission for all, limited tickets guaranteed, save 50%, official city tourism pass. Additional specific forbidden visual caps are derived from visual_rules.json, deprecated_assets.json, and style_guide.md for concrete old-template or forbidden-asset failures.
